purpose: Read Cursor's UZG+ audit cross-published in Uniton_Shared, compare canon claims (project knowledge + whitepaper + product map + module roadmap) against V2 implementation reality (96 migrations, 16 functional domains), produce reconciliation proposal for NTS approval. Also fix HD-03 from Cursor audit by extending sync_runtime_to_public.yml to mirror audits/ecosystem/**, system_maps/**, handoffs/inbox/**.
head_pre_flight: post LANE03 branch protection verify (commit ddb3012) + post LANE01-AIER-CODE-UZG-PLUS-RUNTIME-NAMESPACE-V1 merge (a958c26 + ff6baac post-merge)
merged_via: gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch --admin (admin override per branch protection requires 1 approval)

post_merge_incident.severity: CRITICAL — leaked credential in commit history
post_merge_incident.trigger: GitHub Push Protection blocked sync_runtime_to_public push to Uniton_Shared_Live mirror
post_merge_incident.violation: audits/ecosystem/uzg-plus/LANE01-UZG-PLUS-AUDIT-AND-SYSTEM-MAP-V1_audit.log line 19 contains a real classic GitHub Personal Access Token (ghp_R...) that Cursor accidentally committed in PR #14
post_merge_incident.scope: Token is on Uniton_Shared main branch in commit history. Push protection prevented propagation to Uniton_Shared_Live mirror. The token's blast radius (org-wide classic PAT per Cursor's own comment) is significantly larger than the fine-grained PAT currently in use.
immediate_actions_taken_by_CLAC1: 1. STOPPED sync attempts — did NOT bypass push protection (would publish secret).

2. Updated sync_runtime_to_public.yml to EXCLUDE *_audit.log + *.log under audits/ from the public mirror — operational logs should not propagate to public repo regardless of secrets, this just makes that explicit.
3. Added a strip step in the cp block: find target/audits -type f \( -name '*_audit.log' -o -name '*.log' \) -delete — push-protection safety net.
4. Documented the leak as INC-01 (this entry) for NTS visibility.
5. Did NOT attempt history rewrite — git filter-repo / amend on a published main commit is destructive and out of CLAC1 self-approve scope without explicit NTS authorisation.
required_NTS_actions: 1. ROTATE the leaked token IMMEDIATELY at github.com/settings/tokens — the token ghp_R... must be revoked.

2. Decide on history scrub: BFG repo-cleaner or git filter-repo to remove the secret from Uniton_Shared commit history. This is destructive and rewrites all SHAs — needs coordinated rebase across all clones. Defer to NTS.
3. Until rotated + scrubbed, anyone with read access to Uniton_Shared can use this PAT.
4. Lane_03 review may want to add a pre-commit hook or CI lint that fails commits containing patterns matching gh PAT formats (ghp_, github_pat_).
outstanding_at_handoff_time: Token is still in commit history (not yet rotated)
History scrub deferred to NTS approval
